defs: A bit of wood split off; a splinter.
A peg or pin for plugging a hole, as in a cask; a spile.
A small roll of paper, or slip of wood, used as a lamplighter, etc.
One of the thick laths or poles driven horizontally ahead on top of a set of the main timbering in advancing a level in loose ground.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Spilt (splt); p. pr. & vb. n. Spilling.]

defs: To cover or decorate with slender pieces of wood, metal, ivory, etc.; to inlay.

records.ety: [OE. spillen, usually, to destroy, AS. spillan, spildan, to destroy; akin to Icel. spilla to destroy, Sw. spilla to spill, Dan. spilde, LG. & D. spillen to squander, OHG. spildan.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Spilled (spld), or Spilt (splt); p. pr. & vb. n. Spilling.]

defs: To destroy; to kill; to put an end to.
quotes: And gave him to the queen, all at her will To choose whether she would him save or spill.
Greater glory think [it] to save than spill.

defs: To mar; to injure; to deface; hence, to destroy by misuse; to waste.
quotes: They [the colors] disfigure the stuff and spill the whole workmanship.
Spill not the morning, the quintessence of day, in recreations.

defs: To suffer to fall or run out of a vessel; to lose, or suffer to be scattered; -- applied to fluids and to substances whose particles are small and loose; as, to spill water from a pail; to spill quicksilver from a vessel; to spill powder from a paper; to spill sand or flour.
notes: ☞ Spill differs from pour in expressing accidental loss, -- a loss or waste contrary to purpose.

defs: To cause to flow out and be lost or wasted; to shed, or suffer to be shed, as in battle or in manslaughter; as, a man spills another's blood, or his own blood.
quotes: And to revenge his blood so justly spilt.

defs: To relieve a sail from the pressure of the wind, so that it can be more easily reefed or furled, or to lessen the strain.
To be destroyed, ruined, or wasted; to come to ruin; to perish; to waste.
quotes: That thou wilt suffer innocents to spill.

defs: To be shed; to run over; to fall out, and be lost or wasted.
